turns.content: so this is kinda random but whats quincy like to live in compared to southie do you know anything about it

Quincy and Southie are pretty different vibes. Quincy is more suburban, generally cheaper rent, and has gotten a lot of development in recent years especially around Quincy Center and Wollaston. It's got Red Line access so you can still get into Boston pretty easily. Southie has more of that neighborhood walkability and nightlife scene though. Are you thinking about moving?

yeah I mean sorta my boy chris has a two bedroom in quincy and his roommate is movin out in february so he asked me if I wanted to take the room and the rents like way cheaper than what im payin now in southie

That's worth looking into for sure. How much cheaper are we talking? Southie rents have gotten pretty crazy in the last few years, so a significant savings in Quincy could make a real difference, especially if the place is decent.
like 400 bucks less a month bro which is insane thats almost 5 grand a year id be savin. chris has a nice spot too its right off the red line near wollaston beach which is kinda cool

$400 a month less is a huge difference — that's real money over a year. And Wollaston is a nice area, being near the beach and the Red Line is a solid combo. The commute would depend on where your job sites are though. How would it affect getting to work?
thats the thing most of our jobs are north of the city or in the suburbs so id be addin like 20 minutes to my drive every mornin which kinda sucks. but 400 bucks is 400 bucks you know

Yeah, that's the classic tradeoff — cheaper rent versus longer commute. 20 extra minutes each way adds up to about 3 extra hours a week in the truck. But you're right, $400 a month is significant. Do you know Chris well enough to be roommates? That's the other big factor.
oh yeah chris is solid weve been boys since high school hes chill as hell and he works nights so wed barely even be home at the same time which is kinda ideal actually

Opposite schedules with a roommate is honestly the dream — you basically get the place to yourself most of the time. And living with someone you've known since high school eliminates a lot of the roommate risk. It sounds like a pretty strong option on paper. What's holding you back?
idk man leavin southie would be weird ive been here my whole life basically like all my boys are here the bars I go to are here my dads shop is like 10 minutes away its just home you know

That makes total sense. There's a real comfort in being in the neighborhood you grew up in, where everything and everyone you know is right there. Quincy isn't far — it's not like you're moving to another state — but it's definitely a different feel. Your friends and the bars aren't going anywhere though, you'd just be a short drive or train ride away.

yeah thats true its not like im movin to like california or somethin lol. and danny would be fine he could find another roommate easy everyone wants to live in southie. I just gotta think about it more I havent committed to anythin yet

Take your time with it. You said February is when the room opens up so you've got a couple months to decide. Maybe go visit Chris's place, check out the neighborhood, see how the commute feels on a weekday morning. That might help you figure out if it's the right move or not. No rush.
thats a good idea actually I should go check it out before I decide anythin. its just one of those things where like the money makes sense but the heart says stay in southie you know what I mean lol

100% — head vs. heart decisions are the hardest ones. The good news is there's no wrong answer here. You'd save money in Quincy with a good roommate situation, or you stay in the neighborhood you love. Either way you're fine. Just don't let the deadline sneak up on you — give Chris an answer with enough time for him to find someone else if you pass.
yeah for sure I wouldnt leave him hangin like that. alright bro thanks for talkin it through im gonna go check out the place this weekend probably and see how it feels. appreciate it
Anytime man. Going to see it in person will give you a much better sense of things. Good luck with the decision either way — can't really go wrong. Take it easy!
